hints.description: This company provides a widely used digital payments platform, enabling individuals and businesses to send and receive money globally. Its services include online payments, mobile payments, and point-of-sale solutions, facilitating transactions for e-commerce, in-store purchases, and peer-to-peer transfers. Founded by Peter Thiel and Max Levchin, it has become a dominant force in the digital payments landscape, processing billions of transactions annually.
hints.funFact1: Initially, the company was known as Confinity and its first product was a security software for handheld devices, before pivoting to focus on digital money transfers.
hints.funFact2: In 2013, eBay acquired the company for $1.1 billion, but later spun it off as an independent publicly traded entity in 2015, allowing it to pursue its own growth strategy and compete more effectively.
